div.head: Mr. Seward to Mr. Adams.
div.p: Sir: The President has received the proceedings of a public meeting of the inhabitants of Cobham, in which they express a very earnest interest in the success of this government in the unhappy civil war in which it is engaged, and in the triumph of the sacred cause which is involved in the struggle. These proceedings were transmitted by Mr. Benjamin Scott, who acted as chairman of the assembly.

You are authorized to make known to Mr. Scott the President’s high appreciation of this sympathy with our country in such a manner as shall be consistent with the courtesy due to her Majesty and her Majesty’s government.
Mr. Scott’s address is Heath House, Wexbridge, Surrey, and Chamber of London, Guildhall, E. C.
I am, sir, your obedient servant,
